_comment: Curated catalog of Ollama models providers can serve via /v1/chat/completions. Each entry carries approx on-disk size (Q4-class), a sane minimum unified-memory requirement (`minRamGb` — the model should sit in ~70% of RAM with room for context + the OS), a `type` (text|code|vision), and `tags` capability badges (reasoning, tools, agentic, code, general, fast, vision, grounding). Served at /models/catalog; the installer and `koretex models` filter by the Mac's actual RAM + free disk and show the badges. `primary` marks the model the network most wants. The ceiling is high-end CONSUMER Apple Silicon — the largest entries target an M3 Ultra Mac Studio (256–512GB); nothing here needs datacenter GPUs. VISION (VLM) models are served over the SAME /v1/chat/completions path (image in, text out) and are allowed, but they're kept out of the tok/s hardware metric (image prefill distorts throughput) — give them conservative `minRamGb` floors since image KV needs headroom. Embedding/image-gen models need other endpoints/units and remain intentionally excluded (filtered server-side). A `tag` is normally an Ollama-library name (`qwen3:8b`); it can also be a HuggingFace GGUF via Ollama's passthrough (`hf.co/<org>/<repo>:<QUANT>`) — providers pull those the same way, but note Ollama serves/bills the LOWERCASED id (pricing + points are case-insensitive to match). Grow this list with what customers actually demand.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B
models.desc: Small & fast — light tasks

models.desc: Tiny hybrid-reasoning model with tool calling
